title: Filipino Lumpia with Vegetables
description: A Filipino-style spring roll dish made with a variety of vegetables, served as a snack or appetizer. Lumpia is a popular Filipino street food.

ingredients.name: Lumpia wrapper
ingredients.notes: usually found in the frozen food section or at an Asian market

instructions: In a large pan, heat 1/4 cup of vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the grated carrots and cook for 2-3 minutes, until they start to soften.
Add the shredded cabbage and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5-7 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix together the flour and water to make a paste.
Add the paste to the pan and mix well to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Prepare the lumpia wrappers according to the package instructions.
Place a tablespoon of the vegetable mixture onto the center of a lumpia wrapper.
Brush the edges of the wrapper with water, then fold the wrapper into a triangle and press the edges together to seal.
Heat about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the lumpia to the skillet and fry until they are golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the lumpia from the oil and drain on paper towels.
